benefit_name: Margin money subsidy for new micro units
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (implemented by KVIC / KVIB / DIC)
benefit_summary: Credit-linked back-ended margin money subsidy to help set up new micro enterprises in manufacturing and service/business activities. Bank finances the balance as term loan and working capital.
benefit_calculation_logic: Subsidy as a percentage of project cost. General category: 15pct urban / 25pct rural. Special category (SC/ST/OBC/minorities/women/ex-servicemen/PwD/NER/hill/border/aspirational): 25pct urban / 35pct rural. Max project cost: 50 lakh manufacturing, 20 lakh service/business.
active_status_evidence: Live e-portal accepting applications; revised guideline dated 07.12.2023 on msme.gov.in
notes: Loans up to 10 lakh are collateral-free per RBI norms; loans above are covered under CGTMSE. Max subsidy shown is 35pct of 50 lakh manufacturing project (special category, rural).
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Paired with the PMEGP second-loan upgradation subsidy row; credit component covered by CGTMSE row

benefit_name: Second-loan margin money subsidy for upgradation of existing PMEGP/REGP/MUDRA units
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (implemented by KVIC / KVIB / DIC)
benefit_summary: Second dose of credit-linked subsidy for upgrading well-performing existing PMEGP/REGP/MUDRA units after successful adjustment of the first subsidy and three-year lock-in.
benefit_calculation_logic: 15pct subsidy (20pct for NER/hill) on project cost. Max project cost for upgradation: 1 crore manufacturing (max subsidy 15 lakh, 20 lakh NER/hill); 25 lakh service/business (max subsidy 3.75 lakh, 5 lakh NER/hill).
active_status_evidence: Upgradation component present in revised guideline 07.12.2023; earmarked annual allocation
notes: Only for existing units that completed 3-year lock-in and successfully adjusted first subsidy. Max shown is 20 lakh (NER/hill manufacturing).
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Second component of PMEGP; see PMEGP new-unit row

scheme_or_product_name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
benefit_name: Guarantee cover on collateral-free credit to micro and small enterprises
benefit_summary: Provides guarantee cover to member lending institutions for collateral-free and third-party-guarantee-free credit extended to eligible micro and small enterprises, including manufacturing, services and retail/wholesale trade.
benefit_calculation_logic: Guarantee cover 75pct to 85pct of the amount in default depending on borrower category and loan size, with an additional 5pct enhancement for women entrepreneurs (per Dec 2024 circular). Annual Guarantee Fee from 0.37pct p.a. (revised Apr 2025).
active_status_evidence: CGTMSE FAQs and circulars dated 2024-25 on cgtmse.in; ceiling raised to 10 crore per borrower
notes: Ceiling of guaranteed credit is 10 crore per eligible borrower. Medium enterprises are not covered. Borrower applies via a Member Lending Institution, not directly to CGTMSE. 10pct fee concession for women/SC/ST/ZED/NER/aspirational-district units.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Startups use the separate CGSS scheme (up to 20 crore). Bank guarantee-backed loan products reference this scheme; CGSSD (subordinate debt) is a related distress guarantee to be captured in the credit batch.

scheme_or_product_name: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS)
benefit_name: 25pct capital subsidy on plant and machinery for SC/ST-owned MSEs
benefit_summary: Upfront capital subsidy to SC/ST-owned micro and small enterprises for procurement of plant, machinery and equipment through institutional credit, to promote technology adoption and participation in public procurement.
benefit_calculation_logic: 25pct subsidy on institutional credit availed for plant and machinery/equipment. Overall ceiling to be confirmed against current guidelines.
active_status_evidence: Dedicated live portal sclcss.msme.gov.in with application status and beneficiary lists
notes: Subsidy routed via nodal banks/agencies (SIDBI, NABARD and others). Overall subsidy ceiling needs confirmation from current guidelines.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Special component distinct from the general CLCSS row

scheme_or_product_name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS) - general component
benefit_name: 15pct capital subsidy for technology upgradation (general)
benefit_summary: Upfront capital subsidy to micro and small enterprises for inducting well-established and improved technology in specified sub-sectors/products, linked to a term loan from a Primary Lending Institution.
benefit_calculation_logic: 15pct subsidy on institutional finance up to 1 crore, i.e. subsidy capped at 15 lakh, on eligible plant and machinery in approved sub-sectors.
active_status_evidence: DC-MSME page states the general scheme is under revision; fresh-application window status is unclear
notes: General CLCSS is reported to be under revision and may not be accepting fresh applications. Include only after confirming an open window. SCLCSS (SC/ST) is separately active.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Sits under the CLCS-TUS umbrella; SCLCSS is the special SC/ST component

benefit_name: Subsidy on cost of ZED certification
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (QCI as implementing partner)
benefit_summary: Government bears a large share of the ZED certification fee (Bronze/Silver/Gold) for registered MSMEs, reducing the cost of quality-and-sustainability certification.
benefit_calculation_logic: Subsidy on certification cost: micro 80pct, small 60pct, medium 50pct. Additional 10pct for women/SC/ST-owned or NER/Himalayan/LWE/island/aspirational-district units, plus additional 5pct for SFURTI or MSE-CDP cluster members. Certification cost: Bronze 10000, Silver 40000, Gold 90000.
active_status_evidence: Live zed.msme.gov.in portal with current subsidy structure and online application
notes: Registration is free. Women-owned MSMEs are reported to get certification effectively free across levels. Max rupee value varies by level and category; compute from the percentage grid.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of the ZED scheme along with joining reward, handholding/tech support and testing reimbursement rows

benefit_name: ZED pledge joining reward
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (QCI as implementing partner)
benefit_summary: One-time limited-purpose reward of 10000 to each MSME on taking the ZED pledge, to be used within a defined period for a defined purpose.
benefit_calculation_logic: Flat 10000 per MSME on taking the ZED pledge; valid for a limited period after the pledge.
active_status_evidence: Joining reward described in current ZED FAQs on zed.msme.gov.in
notes: Added benefits reported for SC/ST and women-led units. Limited-purpose and time-bound.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of the ZED scheme; see other ZED rows

benefit_name: Handholding and zero-effect technology upgradation support
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (QCI as implementing partner)
benefit_summary: Financial support of up to 5 lakh per ZED-certified MSME for consultancy handholding and for moving towards zero-effect solutions, pollution control or cleaner technology.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 5 lakh per MSME (up to 2 lakh for handholding/consultancy and up to 3 lakh for zero-effect technology), subsidized at micro 80pct, small 60pct, medium 50pct, with the same additional 10pct/5pct enhancements as certification.
active_status_evidence: Handholding/tech-upgradation support described on current zed.msme.gov.in page
notes: Requires at least ZED Bronze certification. Tech-upgradation portion is subject to consultant recommendation, MSME-DI verification and PMAC approval.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of the ZED scheme; see other ZED rows

benefit_name: Reimbursement for testing / system / product certification
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (QCI as implementing partner)
benefit_summary: After achieving a ZED certification level, MSMEs can claim reimbursement on the cost of one testing, one management-system certification or one product certification.
benefit_calculation_logic: Subsidy on cost of one Testing OR one Management System OR one Product Certification after achieving a ZED level. Rupee cap to be confirmed from scheme guidelines (secondary sources cite up to 50000).
active_status_evidence: Testing/certification reimbursement confirmed in ZED FAQs; exact cap from secondary source
notes: Management-system certification must be through an NABCB-accredited body. Applicable only after achieving a ZED level. Rupee cap needs official confirmation.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of the ZED scheme; see other ZED rows

benefit_name: Subsidy on Lean implementation and consultancy cost
benefit_summary: Government contributes the bulk of the cost of Lean manufacturing implementation (5S, Kaizen, Kanban and similar tools) through empanelled consultants across Basic, Intermediate and Advanced levels.
benefit_calculation_logic: 90pct of implementation/consultancy cost borne by GoI, plus an additional 5pct for women/SC/ST-owned, SFURTI-cluster or NER units, plus an additional 5pct for units registering via Industry Associations/OEMs after completing all levels. 5000 per MSME to the OEM/Association on completion.
active_status_evidence: lean.msme.gov.in shows financial assistance; but scheme guideline validity was stated till 31 March 2026
notes: Scheme validity was stated up to 31 March 2026; confirm extension/continuation and per-level rupee caps (secondary sources cite up to 1.2 lakh Intermediate and 2.4 lakh Advanced).
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: none

scheme_or_product_name: MSME Innovative Scheme (Incubation, Design and IPR)
benefit_name: Incubation support for innovative ideas
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (via recognized Host Institutes)
benefit_summary: Financial assistance routed through recognized Host Institutes to develop and nurture innovative ideas of MSMEs and innovators, covering prototype development and commercialization support.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 15 lakh per approved innovative idea to the Host Institute for developing/nurturing the idea. Separately, up to 1 crore to a Host Institute for plant and machinery to strengthen incubation facilities.
active_status_evidence: Live innovative.msme.gov.in portal; MSME Idea Hackathons run as the selection route
notes: Individuals/students must apply through a recognized Host Institute. The 15 lakh per idea is a grant, not a loan. Up to 1 crore infra support is to the Host Institute, not the MSME.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Amalgamation of erstwhile Incubation, Design and IPR schemes; see Design, IPR and equity rows

scheme_or_product_name: MSME Innovative Scheme (Incubation, Design and IPR)
benefit_name: Design project financial assistance
benefit_summary: Financial assistance for engaging professional design expertise for new product development and improvement of existing products, plus support for student design projects.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 40 lakh per design project for individual MSMEs (75pct for micro, 60pct for small/medium). Student design projects up to 2.5 lakh. Disbursed in phases (40pct concept, 30pct detailed design, 30pct post-prototype).
active_status_evidence: Design component described on current innovative.msme.gov.in and scheme guidelines
notes: Beneficiary contribution is the balance of the project cost (25pct micro, 40pct small/medium). Student projects capped at 2.5 lakh.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of MSME Innovative Scheme; see Incubation, IPR and equity rows

scheme_or_product_name: MSME Innovative Scheme (Incubation, Design and IPR)
benefit_name: Reimbursement for IPR registration (patent, trademark, GI, design)
benefit_summary: One-time reimbursement of expenditure incurred by MSMEs on registering patents, trademarks, geographical indications and designs, to strengthen IP protection and commercialization.
benefit_calculation_logic: Reimbursement caps: foreign patent up to 5 lakh, domestic patent up to 1 lakh, GI registration up to 2 lakh, design registration up to 15000, trademark up to 10000. One-time, not claimable if already claimed under another government scheme.
active_status_evidence: Live IPR reimbursement portal innovative.msme.gov.in/Home/IprIndex
notes: Valid Udyam registration required; claim after grant/registration of the IP, typically within one financial year.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of MSME Innovative Scheme; see Incubation, Design and equity rows

scheme_or_product_name: MSME Innovative Scheme (Incubation, Design and IPR)
benefit_name: Equity support for commercialization of ideas/designs/patents
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (corpus managed by SIDBI)
benefit_summary: Equity support to help MSMEs commercialize proven ideas, designs and patents and scale up to raise subsequent funding, through a corpus managed by SIDBI.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 1 crore equity support per case, with GoI contribution up to 80pct (80:20 ratio), managed by SIDBI as fund manager.
active_status_evidence: Equity corpus described in guidelines; operational launch/availability needs confirmation
notes: Some sources indicate the equity corpus was yet to be operationalized. Confirm current availability with SIDBI/Ministry before treating as open.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of MSME Innovative Scheme; see Incubation, Design and IPR rows

scheme_or_product_name: Micro and Small Enterprises Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP)
benefit_name: Grant for Common Facility Centre (CFC)
benefit_summary: One-time capital grant to a Special Purpose Vehicle of clustered micro and small enterprises for setting up a Common Facility Centre (shared plant, machinery, testing, plug-and-play facilities).
benefit_calculation_logic: GoI grant is a percentage of project cost with a ceiling; assistance is 90pct of project cost for CFC projects in NER/hilly states, island territories, aspirational/LWE districts and for special-category groups, subject to a GoI-share ceiling (commonly cited at 5 crore for CFC). Land cost counts up to 25pct of project cost.
active_status_evidence: Live cluster.dcmsme.gov.in portal and current MSE-CDP guidelines PDF on dcmsme.gov.in
notes: Delivered at cluster/SPV level; individual MSEs benefit collectively via the shared facility. Infrastructure Development component supports industrial estates/flatted factory complexes (larger ceilings). Thematic interventions: 50pct grant up to 2 lakh per activity.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Cluster-level scheme; ZED/Lean give extra subsidy to MSE-CDP cluster members

benefit_name: Financial assistance for market access (trade fairs, packaging, barcode)
benefit_summary: Financial support to MSEs for participating in domestic and international trade fairs/exhibitions and for adopting bar coding and modern packaging, to improve market access and marketability.
benefit_calculation_logic: Reimbursement-based support for eligible market-access activities (e.g., stall/space with financial support calculated for up to 9 sqm, participation/travel, one-time barcode registration fee). Rates vary by activity and category; confirm current caps from guidelines.
active_status_evidence: Scheme listed on dcmsme.gov.in marketing assistance page; revised PMS guidelines referenced
notes: A component under Development of MSMEs (Entrepreneurship and Skill Development). Confirm current activity-wise reimbursement caps and open windows from the latest guidelines.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related to NSIC marketing/procurement support (to be captured in the sectoral-institutions batch)

scheme_or_product_name: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries (SFURTI)
benefit_name: Cluster development financial assistance for traditional industries
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (Nodal Agencies: KVIC, Coir Board, DC-MSME)
benefit_summary: Cluster-based financial assistance to organize artisans and traditional-industry units into clusters, funding Common Facility Centres, raw material banks, tools/technology upgradation, skilling and market promotion via a Special Purpose Vehicle.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 2.5 crore for regular clusters (up to 500 artisans) and up to 5 crore for major clusters (more than 500 artisans). GoI funds 90pct of hard interventions for special-category/NER clusters and 75pct for others; 20pct of hard-intervention cost goes to a working-capital corpus.
active_status_evidence: Live sfurti.msme.gov.in project management portal; revised guidelines available
notes: Delivered via Implementing Agencies/SPVs (NGOs, cooperatives, producer companies); individual artisans/micro-enterprises benefit collectively. Individual PMEGP beneficiaries are not directly eligible. Min 100 artisans (50 in hilly/NER).
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Traditional-industry cluster scheme; nodal overlap with KVIC and Coir Board schemes to be captured in the sectoral-institutions batch

benefit_name: Collateral-free micro loan (Shishu, Kishore, Tarun)
provider_name: MUDRA (refinance) via Member Lending Institutions; Ministry of Finance
benefit_summary: Collateral-free loans for income-generating non-corporate, non-farm micro enterprises, delivered by banks, RRBs, SFBs, NBFCs and MFIs. Covers term loan and working capital across three graded categories.
benefit_calculation_logic: Shishu: up to 50000. Kishore: above 50000 up to 5 lakh. Tarun: above 5 lakh up to 10 lakh. Loans are collateral-free and guaranteed under the Credit Guarantee Fund for Micro Units (CGFMU) managed by NCGTC.
active_status_evidence: mudra.org.in live; online applications via udyamimitra.in and jansamarth.in
notes: MUDRA is a refinance institution and does not lend directly. Interest varies by lender per RBI norms. Working capital can be operated via a MUDRA Card overdraft.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Tarun Plus (10-20 lakh) is a separate row. Guarantee is via CGFMU, distinct from CGTMSE.

benefit_name: Tarun Plus collateral-free loan (10 lakh to 20 lakh)
provider_name: MUDRA (refinance) via Member Lending Institutions; Ministry of Finance
benefit_summary: Higher-ticket collateral-free MUDRA loan introduced in October 2024 for micro entrepreneurs who have already availed and fully repaid a Tarun-category loan, raising the PMMY ceiling to 20 lakh.
benefit_calculation_logic: Loans above 10 lakh and up to 20 lakh. Available only to borrowers who previously availed and successfully repaid a Tarun loan. Guaranteed under CGFMU.
active_status_evidence: Limit enhancement notified 24 October 2024 (Union Budget 2024-25); confirmed on mudra.org.in and PIB
notes: Repeat-borrower category; not open to all applicants. CGFMU guarantee extended to cover the enhanced limit.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Extension of PMMY; see the Shishu/Kishore/Tarun row

benefit_name: Composite bank loan for SC/ST and women greenfield enterprises
provider_name: Ministry of Finance (Department of Financial Services); SIDBI refinance
benefit_summary: Facilitates a composite bank loan (term loan plus working capital) to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch to set up a first-time (greenfield) enterprise.
benefit_calculation_logic: Composite loan between 10 lakh and 1 crore, covering up to 85pct of project cost (borrower brings minimum 10pct own contribution; 15pct margin can converge with other central/state schemes). Interest capped at the bank's lowest applicable rate not exceeding MCLR + 3pct + tenor premium.
active_status_evidence: standupmitra.in live scheme features page; scheme extended and operational
notes: Greenfield (first-time venture) only. Non-individual entities need 51pct SC/ST or woman shareholding. Backed by Credit Guarantee Fund for Stand-Up India (CGFSI) or bank collateral at bank discretion. Rupay debit card issued.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: A new Budget 2025-26 scheme for first-time SC/ST women entrepreneurs (term loans up to 2 crore) is separate and noted as uncertain/emerging.

benefit_name: Collateral-free credit guarantee for DPIIT-recognised startups
provider_name: NCGTC (DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce and Industry)
benefit_summary: Provides collateral-free credit guarantee cover to banks, NBFCs and SEBI-registered venture debt funds lending to DPIIT-recognised startups, across venture debt, working capital, term loans and other debt instruments.
benefit_calculation_logic: Guarantee ceiling 20 crore per borrower (revised 2025). Extent of cover: 85pct of amount in default for loans up to 10 crore, 75pct for loans above 10 crore. Annual Guarantee Fee 1pct p.a. for 27 Champion Sectors, otherwise 2pct (1.5pct for women/NER).
active_status_evidence: DPIIT notification dated 08.05.2025 raising ceiling to 20 crore; live NCGTC and Startup India pages
notes: Startup must be DPIIT-recognised, not in default/NPA. Guarantee issued via NCGTC to Member Institutions; borrowers apply through the lender. Overlaps DPIIT/Startup India scope but placed here as a credit guarantee.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Startup counterpart to CGTMSE (which covers MSEs up to 10 crore). See CGTMSE row in the Ministry of MSME batch.

scheme_or_product_name: SIDBI Make in India Soft Loan Fund for MSMEs (SMILE)
benefit_name: Soft loan / quasi-equity for new and expanding MSMEs
benefit_summary: Direct SIDBI assistance combining a term loan with a soft-loan (quasi-equity) component to help new and existing MSMEs in manufacturing and services set up, expand, modernise or upgrade technology, including meeting the promoter contribution gap.
benefit_calculation_logic: Minimum loan reported at 10 lakh (equipment) and 25 lakh (other purposes); soft-loan component functions as quasi-equity. Concessional interest reported around 2pct below market. Long repayment (up to about 10 years) with moratorium up to 36 months. Confirm current terms from SIDBI product sheet.
active_status_evidence: SMILE listed among SIDBI direct products on sidbi.in; specific figures from secondary sources
notes: Flagship SIDBI direct soft-loan scheme. Exact current ceilings, rate and tenure should be confirmed from the live SIDBI product sheet before publication.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: One of several SIDBI direct products (also SEF, SPEED, STAR, 4E, ARISE, Express, PRAYAAS)

benefit_name: Collateral-free finance for MSME traders/retailers/wholesalers
benefit_summary: Direct SIDBI working-capital and need-based finance for MSME traders such as stockists, distributors, dealers and organised retailers, offered without collateral.
benefit_calculation_logic: Loan reported from about 10 lakh up to 1 crore per borrower for working capital and need-based capital expenditure; repayment up to about 60 months. Applicant should be a trader in existence for at least 3 years with satisfactory financials. Confirm current terms from SIDBI.
active_status_evidence: STFS described on SIDBI product pages; scheme parameters from secondary sources
notes: Traders already availing working-capital loans from other banks are generally not eligible. Confirm current ceiling, rate and eligibility from SIDBI before publication.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: One of several SIDBI direct products

scheme_or_product_name: SIDBI Loan for Purchase of Equipment for Enterprise's Development (SPEED)
benefit_name: Equipment finance (up to 100pct financing)
benefit_summary: Direct SIDBI term loan for purchase of new machinery and equipment, offering high (up to full) financing of the equipment cost to help MSMEs modernise.
benefit_calculation_logic: Reported up to 100pct financing, with loan up to about 1 crore for newer businesses and up to about 2 crore for enterprises operational 3+ years; repayment about 2-5 years with 3-6 month moratorium; interest around 9-10pct. Confirm current terms from SIDBI.
active_status_evidence: SPEED listed among SIDBI direct products; figures from secondary/older sources
notes: A SPEED PLUS variant is also reported. Exact current financing percentage, ceiling, rate and tenure need confirmation from SIDBI product sheets.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: One of several SIDBI direct products; related to SEF (SMILE Equipment Finance)

benefit_name: Micro-enterprise finance via partner institutions
benefit_summary: SIDBI-linked lending program that channels affordable credit to micro-entrepreneurs, including women-led enterprises, through partner institutions rather than direct SIDBI branches.
benefit_calculation_logic: Need-based micro loans delivered via SIDBI partner institutions; exact ticket size, rate and tenure vary by partner and are not published centrally. Confirm current terms from SIDBI.
active_status_evidence: PRAYAAS referenced as an active SIDBI micro-lending initiative; central terms not published
notes: Delivered through partner institutions; discoverability and terms are partner-dependent. A Digital PRAYAAS variant has also been reported. Needs confirmation from SIDBI.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: One of several SIDBI direct/partner products

benefit_name: Seed grant for proof of concept / prototype / product trials
benefit_summary: Milestone-based seed grant disbursed to DPIIT-recognised early-stage startups through selected incubators, to validate proof of concept, develop prototypes or run product trials.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 20 lakh as a grant, released in milestone-based instalments (prototype, product testing, market-ready product). A startup can avail the grant once. Not for facility creation.
active_status_evidence: Live seedfund.startupindia.gov.in portal with ongoing applications; disbursals reported through 2024-25
notes: Startup must be DPIIT-recognised and incorporated not more than 2 years ago at application; applicant may apply to up to 3 incubators. Should not have received more than 10 lakh under other central/state schemes.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Paired with the SISFS debt/convertible-debenture row (up to 50 lakh)

benefit_name: Debt / convertible debentures for market entry and scaling
benefit_summary: Investment through convertible debentures or debt / debt-linked instruments to DPIIT-recognised startups (via incubators) for market entry, commercialization or scaling up.
benefit_calculation_logic: Up to 50 lakh via convertible debentures or debt/debt-linked instruments. Unsecured (no collateral/guarantee), typically low interest, tenure set by incubator up to about 5 years, with a moratorium (about 12 months). Availed once per startup.
active_status_evidence: Debt/convertible component described in current SISFS guidelines and FAQ on the official portal
notes: Used for market entry/commercialization/scaling only, not facility creation. Disbursed by the selected incubator, not the government directly.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Second instrument under SISFS; see the grant row

benefit_name: Indirect equity capital via SEBI-registered AIFs
benefit_summary: Government-backed fund-of-funds that contributes capital to SEBI-registered Category I and II Alternative Investment Funds, which in turn invest equity in DPIIT-recognised startups. Startups do not apply directly.
benefit_calculation_logic: Corpus of 10,000 crore contributed to AIFs; each AIF must invest at least twice the FFS contribution into eligible startups. FFS typically contributes up to about 20pct of an AIF's corpus. Benefit to a startup is the equity investment received from a participating AIF (amount varies).
active_status_evidence: sidbivcf.in shows FFS status as on 31 Dec 2025; commitments exceed 11,000 crore across 140+ AIFs
notes: Indirect scheme: only SEBI-registered AIFs apply to SIDBI; startups access capital by approaching participating AIFs. A successor FFS 2.0 (additional 10,000 crore, focus deep-tech/climate/defence) has been notified with SIDBI as implementation agency.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Equity counterpart to SISFS (grant/debt) and CGSS (guarantee) in the startup financing stack

scheme_or_product_name: Interest Subvention Support under Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan)
benefit_name: Interest subvention on pre and post shipment rupee export credit (MSME manufacturers)
provider_name: DGFT (Ministry of Commerce and Industry); RBI reimbursement to banks
benefit_summary: Reduction in the interest rate on pre- and post-shipment rupee export credit for eligible MSME manufacturer exporters, continuing the erstwhile Interest Equalisation Scheme under the new Export Promotion Mission framework.
benefit_calculation_logic: Interest subvention of about 2.75pct (historically 3pct) per annum for MSME manufacturer exporters on rupee export credit, subject to an annual per-MSME ceiling (a 50 lakh cap applied in FY 2024-25). Current rate and cap governed by DGFT trade notices under the Export Promotion Mission (Jan 2026).
active_status_evidence: DGFT Trade Notice 22/2025-26 dated 16 Jan 2026 governs interest subvention under EPM; export credit sanctioned on/after 02 Jan 2026 qualifies
notes: Successor to the Interest Equalisation Scheme (IES), now under the Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan, outlay 25,060 crore FY26-31). Requires a DGFT-generated UIN submitted to the bank; goods must originate in India. Confirm current rate and annual cap from the latest DGFT trade notice.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Replaces the standalone IES; EPM also bundles export credit guarantee and factoring (verify separately)

scheme_or_product_name: Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RoDTEP)
benefit_name: Remission of embedded duties/taxes on exported goods
provider_name: DGFT / CBIC (Ministry of Commerce and Industry; Ministry of Finance)
benefit_summary: Refund of embedded central, state and local duties and taxes on exported products that are not otherwise refunded (for example VAT on fuel, mandi tax, electricity duty), issued as transferable e-scrips/ledger credits.
benefit_calculation_logic: Remission as a percentage of FOB value of exports, varying roughly from 0.5pct to 4.3pct by product HS code as notified. Credited as transferable duty credit in the exporter's electronic ledger on ICEGATE.
active_status_evidence: RoDTEP is the current flagship remission scheme under FTP 2023 (successor to MEIS); processed via ICEGATE
notes: Available to almost all export sectors; reported that a large majority of beneficiaries are MSMEs. Exact per-product rates are notified on the DGFT/ICEGATE portal and should be checked by HS code before publication.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Textile-specific RoSCTL offers separate (often higher) rates and is deferred to the sectoral batch

benefit_name: Concessional/zero customs duty on capital goods against export obligation
benefit_summary: Allows exporters to import capital goods (machinery, spares, moulds, software) at zero or concessional customs duty, in exchange for a commitment to export goods worth a multiple of the duty saved.
benefit_calculation_logic: Duty saved on imported capital goods, conditional on fulfilling an export obligation equal to 6 times the duty saved within 6 years (export obligation reduced by 25pct for indigenously sourced capital goods). IGST exemption also available on EPCG imports.
active_status_evidence: Long-standing scheme (since 1992) continuing under Foreign Trade Policy 2023; administered by DGFT
notes: Benefit is a duty saving, not a cash transfer; failure to meet the export obligation triggers duty recovery with interest. Confirm current duty rates and EO conditions from FTP/HBP before publication.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related duty schemes (Advance Authorisation, Duty Drawback) are broad trade facilitation and noted in methodology

benefit_name: Financial assistance for export market development
provider_name: Department of Commerce (Ministry of Commerce and Industry)
benefit_summary: Financial assistance for export-promotion activities (market studies, participation in international fairs, buyer-seller meets, branding and market development), delivered largely via Export Promotion Councils and trade bodies, benefiting MSME exporters entering new markets.
benefit_calculation_logic: Reimbursement/financial support for eligible export-promotion activities on a component-wise basis as per scheme guidelines; specific caps vary by activity and applicant category.
active_status_evidence: MAI listed among active export promotion schemes; some activities are being reorganised under the Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Disha)
notes: Often accessed through Export Promotion Councils/trade bodies rather than by individual MSMEs directly. Market-access support is being consolidated under the Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Disha); confirm the current operative guidelines and caps.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related to TMA (Transport and Marketing Assistance) and Niryat Disha market-access components

benefit_name: Credit for raw material procurement against bank guarantee
benefit_summary: NSIC finances the purchase of raw materials (indigenous and imported) for manufacturing/processing MSMEs on credit, letting them buy in bulk and focus on production. Trading activity is excluded.
benefit_calculation_logic: Credit for raw material for up to about 90 days (extendable up to 180 days) against a bank guarantee. Concessional interest linked to the unit's rating (for example about 9.00pct p.a. for a micro unit with a valid SME-1 rating), typically ranging up to about 9.5-12pct.
active_status_evidence: RMA described on nsic.co.in as a current credit-support scheme; applications via NSIC branches
notes: Requires a bank guarantee as security. Only for manufacturing/service MSMEs with valid Udyam registration, not trading. Confirm current interest grid and sanction limits from NSIC.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: One of several NSIC financial-support schemes (see Bank Credit Facilitation and SPRS rows)

benefit_name: Facilitated access to bank credit (fund and non-fund based)
provider_name: NSIC (Ministry of MSME), with partner banks
benefit_summary: NSIC helps MSMEs obtain fund-based and non-fund-based credit limits from tie-up banks by preparing documentation and following up with banks, at no cost to the MSME.
benefit_calculation_logic: Credit is sanctioned by the partner bank (NSIC facilitates, does not lend). Reported facilitation of loans up to about 5 crore for a single unit and up to about 15 crore for a group of units; interest per bank (about 9.5-12pct).
active_status_evidence: Bank Credit Facilitation Scheme page live on nsic.co.in
notes: This is a facilitation service (free handholding) rather than a direct subsidy; the actual loan and its terms come from the partner bank. Included because it directly unlocks a financial benefit.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Complements CGTMSE and bank MSME products captured elsewhere

benefit_name: Procurement-linked financial benefits (EMD exemption, free tenders)
benefit_summary: Registration that lets micro and small enterprises participate in government and PSU tenders with financial concessions such as exemption from Earnest Money Deposit, free issue of tender documents, and participation without prior turnover/experience conditions.
benefit_calculation_logic: Non-monetary-value benefit realised as cost savings: EMD exemption, free tender sets, and tender participation on par with larger firms. Registration is free of cost for micro and small enterprises.
active_status_evidence: SPRS described as active on NSIC pages; long-standing scheme under the Government Stores Purchase Programme
notes: Financial value is indirect (avoided EMD and tender costs, and access to reserved-item procurement/price preference under the Public Procurement Policy). Registration free for MSEs.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Complements the Public Procurement Policy for MSEs (25pct procurement mandate)

benefit_name: Concessional 4pct interest on credit to khadi institutions
benefit_summary: Registered khadi institutions receive bank credit for capital investment and working capital at a concessional 4pct interest rate; the difference above 4pct is paid by the Government through KVIC directly to the bank.
benefit_calculation_logic: Institution pays only 4pct per annum; KVIC pays the balance interest to the bank from its Grants budget. Applies to Capital Investment and Working Capital based on production levels and assessed requirement.
active_status_evidence: ISEC described as active in KVIC/PIB release (2025) under Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana
notes: Available to KVIC-registered khadi/polyvastra institutions (not individuals directly). Part of the Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana umbrella.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Umbrella: Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana (also MMDA, Workshed)

scheme_or_product_name: Modified Market Development Assistance (MMDA) for Khadi
benefit_name: Production/marketing assistance on prime cost of khadi
benefit_summary: Financial assistance calculated on the prime cost of khadi production, distributed among selling institutions, producing institutions and artisans, with a share passed directly to artisans/spinners/weavers as an income incentive.
benefit_calculation_logic: Assistance at a percentage of prime cost: reported at 35pct for cotton/muslin/wool/polyvastra khadi and 20pct for silk khadi (structures have varied across MDA/MPDA/MMDA versions). A defined share is credited directly to artisans as incentive.
active_status_evidence: MMDA described as current KVIC assistance in 2025 PIB release; exact percentages from secondary sources
notes: Percentage rates and distribution split have changed across MDA/MPDA/MMDA versions; confirm the current MMDA rate and split from KVIC guidelines.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana (see ISEC, Workshed)

benefit_name: Financial assistance for construction of artisan worksheds
benefit_summary: Financial assistance to khadi artisans (with focus on BPL/weaker sections) to construct improved worksheds for spinning and weaving, including storage for raw materials, tools and finished goods.
benefit_calculation_logic: Grant assistance for workshed construction routed via the khadi institution; per-artisan assistance amount as prescribed in KVIC guidelines (to be confirmed).
active_status_evidence: Listed as a current KVIC artisan-welfare scheme in 2025 PIB release; per-unit amount not stated
notes: Delivered through khadi institutions to artisans (focus on BPL). Confirm current per-artisan assistance amount from KVIC.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of Khadi Gramodyog Vikas Yojana; KVIC also runs Gramodyog Vikas Yojana toolkit programmes (Honey Mission, Kumhar Sashaktikaran) noted in methodology

benefit_name: Credit-linked capital subsidy for coir units
benefit_summary: Credit-linked subsidy to set up coir production/processing units, combining a government subsidy, a bank loan and a small beneficiary contribution.
benefit_calculation_logic: Pattern: 40pct of project cost as GoI subsidy, 55pct bank loan, 5pct beneficiary contribution. Maximum admissible project cost 10 lakh plus one cycle of working capital (WC not exceeding 25pct of project cost; WC not subsidised). Max subsidy therefore about 4 lakh.
active_status_evidence: Coir Board financial-assistance page and online portal (coirservices.gov.in) current
notes: Formerly REMOT scheme. EDP training mandatory. No collateral per RBI norms for eligible loans. One person per family. No income ceiling.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Coir Board also runs Coir Vikas Yojana (Production Infrastructure) and Mahila Coir Yojana rows

scheme_or_product_name: Coir Vikas Yojana - Development of Production Infrastructure
benefit_name: Subsidy on equipment/infrastructure for coir units
benefit_summary: Subsidy towards the cost of equipment and infrastructure for setting up or modernising coir units (defibering, automatic spinning and others).
benefit_calculation_logic: 25pct of the cost of equipment and infrastructure, subject to ceilings: up to 6 lakh (defibering unit), 4 lakh (automatic spinning unit), 5 lakh (others), and 9 lakh (composite/multiple unit). For modernisation, 25pct subject to a maximum of 2 lakh.
active_status_evidence: Described on Coir Board financial-assistance page as a current scheme
notes: Ceilings vary by unit type; max shown is the composite/multiple-unit ceiling (9 lakh). Modernisation capped at 2 lakh.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of Coir Vikas Yojana; see CUY and Mahila Coir Yojana rows

benefit_name: 75pct subsidy on coir processing equipment for rural women
benefit_summary: After training, rural women artisans receive motorised ratts and other coir-processing equipment at a 75pct subsidy, plus a training-period stipend, to enable home-based coir livelihoods.
benefit_calculation_logic: 75pct subsidy on the cost of a motorised ratt/traditional ratt and other equipment, subject to a maximum of 7,500 per beneficiary, after completing a 2-month training programme (stipend of 1,000 per month during training).
active_status_evidence: Described on Coir Board financial-assistance page as a current women-artisan scheme
notes: Equipment subsidy is small (max 7,500) but paired with a monthly training stipend of 1,000 for 2 months. Exclusive to rural women artisans in coir-producing regions.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of Coir Vikas Yojana (Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana)

scheme_or_product_name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
benefit_name: Credit-linked capital subsidy for individual micro food units
benefit_summary: Credit-linked capital subsidy to individual micro food processing units for setting up or upgrading, with a preference for One District One Product (ODOP) products.
benefit_calculation_logic: 35pct of eligible project cost as credit-linked capital subsidy, capped at 10 lakh per unit. Beneficiary brings minimum 10pct; balance is a bank loan. Repayment up to about 10 years with 6-24 month moratorium.
active_status_evidence: 1,62,744 credit-linked-subsidy loans sanctioned by 31 Oct 2025 (PIB); live pmfme.mofpi.gov.in portal
notes: Original scheme period was 2020-21 to 2024-25 with a continuation into 2025-26; confirm current window. ODOP proposals preferred but non-ODOP also eligible. Union Bank of India is the nodal bank.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: PMFME also provides SHG seed capital and common-infrastructure subsidy (separate rows)

scheme_or_product_name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
benefit_name: Seed capital to SHG members in food processing
benefit_summary: Seed capital provided to members of Self-Help Groups engaged in food processing, for working capital and purchase of small tools, routed through the SHG federation.
benefit_calculation_logic: Seed capital of 40,000 per SHG member engaged in food processing, subject to a maximum of 4 lakh per SHG federation. Provided as a grant to the federation, extended to members as a loan through the SHG.
active_status_evidence: Seed-capital component described in current PMFME guidelines and PIB release
notes: 40,000 per member is a grant to the SHG federation, on-lent to members via the SHG. Distinct from the individual credit-linked subsidy.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Component of PMFME; see individual subsidy and common-infrastructure rows

scheme_or_product_name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
benefit_name: Credit-linked grant for common infrastructure and branding/marketing
benefit_summary: Credit-linked grant to FPOs, SHGs, cooperatives and government agencies for common infrastructure (processing, storage, packaging, incubation), plus marketing and branding support for group beneficiaries.
benefit_calculation_logic: 35pct credit-linked grant for common infrastructure, subject to a maximum of 3 crore. Branding and marketing support to FPOs/SHGs/cooperatives/SPVs at 50pct of total expenditure (with DPR support up to 5 lakh from the State Nodal Agency).
active_status_evidence: Common-infrastructure and branding components described in current PMFME guidelines and PIB release
notes: Common infrastructure must be available to other units on a hiring basis for a substantial part of capacity. NAFED/TRIFED support branding and marketing.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Component of PMFME; see individual subsidy and SHG seed-capital rows

scheme_or_product_name: Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI)
benefit_name: Incentive on incremental sales of processed food
benefit_summary: Performance-linked incentive paid on incremental sales of eligible processed-food products (and for branding/marketing abroad), aimed primarily at larger food processing companies but with some components relevant to growing MSMEs.
benefit_calculation_logic: Incentive computed as a percentage of incremental sales over a base year, subject to eligibility thresholds on minimum sales and committed investment; separate support for branding/marketing abroad. Thresholds generally favour mid-to-large firms.
active_status_evidence: PLISFPI is an approved MoFPI scheme; application windows are periodic and eligibility is threshold-based
notes: Primarily targets larger food processing firms (minimum sales/investment thresholds); MSME applicability is limited to specific components (for example millet-based products, SME/innovative categories). Confirm current window and thresholds.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Complements PMFME (which targets micro units); different instrument (incentive vs subsidy)

scheme_or_product_name: Agri Clinics and Agri Business Centres (ACABC) Scheme
benefit_name: Credit-linked composite subsidy for agri-entrepreneurs
provider_name: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare (NABARD as subsidy channelising agency)
benefit_summary: Supports agriculture graduates/diploma holders to set up agri-clinics and agri-business ventures, providing free training and handholding plus a bank loan with a back-ended credit-linked composite subsidy.
benefit_calculation_logic: Credit-linked back-ended composite subsidy of 36pct of project cost for general category and 44pct for SC/ST, women, NER and hill-area candidates. Individual project ceiling commonly cited around 20 lakh (higher for group ventures). NABARD channels the subsidy.
active_status_evidence: NABARD page shows FY2024-25 continuation circular and 2025 revised programme instructions; beneficiary list to 31.03.2025
notes: Eligibility limited to agriculture/allied graduates, diploma or PG holders as specified. Confirm current subsidy ceiling and project-cost limits from the 2025 revised guidelines.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Ministry of Agriculture scheme with NABARD as channelising agency; relevant to agri-allied MSMEs

benefit_name: 3pct interest subvention and guarantee for agri-infrastructure
provider_name: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare (NABARD/banks as lenders)
benefit_summary: Financing facility for post-harvest management and community farming assets and agri-processing infrastructure, offering an interest subvention and credit-guarantee support on eligible loans.
benefit_calculation_logic: Interest subvention of 3pct per annum on loans up to 2 crore, for up to 7 years. Credit-guarantee cover available (for example via CGTMSE) on eligible loans up to 2 crore. Loans above 2 crore are eligible for the facility without the subvention on the excess.
active_status_evidence: AIF is an ongoing central scheme with a live application portal (agriinfra.dac.gov.in)
notes: Eligible beneficiaries include FPOs, agri-entrepreneurs, startups, primary processing units and others. Relevant to agri-allied and food-processing MSMEs. Confirm current subvention cap and eligible-activity list.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Complements PMFME for food-processing/storage infrastructure

scheme_or_product_name: Rebate of State and Central Taxes and Levies (RoSCTL) Scheme
benefit_name: Rebate of embedded state/central taxes on apparel and made-ups exports
provider_name: Ministry of Textiles (DGFT for scrip issuance)
benefit_summary: Rebate of embedded state and central taxes and levies on exports of readymade garments/apparel and made-ups, issued as transferable, saleable duty-credit scrips based on FOB value.
benefit_calculation_logic: Rebate as a percentage of FOB value per notified schedule; reported maximum around 6.05pct for apparel and 8.2pct for made-ups. Claimed via online ANF 4R on the DGFT portal with a digital signature. Textile products not covered under RoSCTL fall under RoDTEP.
active_status_evidence: Ministry of Textiles confirmed RoSCTL as being implemented for apparel/made-ups (Rajya Sabha reply, 20 Mar 2026)
notes: Textile-specific counterpart to RoDTEP, generally at higher rates. Confirm current per-item rates from the notified RoSCTL schedule.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related to RoDTEP (C3) which covers non-RoSCTL textile lines

benefit_name: Export credit insurance cover for exporters and banks
provider_name: ECGC Ltd (Ministry of Commerce and Industry)
benefit_summary: Export credit insurance from ECGC that protects exporters against non-payment by overseas buyers and protects banks lending export credit, improving MSME exporters' access to and cost of credit.
benefit_calculation_logic: Cover up to about 90pct of principal and interest for banks on export credit; enhanced 90pct cover for banks on export credit loans up to 50 crore (raised from 20 crore) effective 01.10.2025; collateral-free cover under WT-ECIB; concessional premiums for small exporters.
active_status_evidence: ECGC enhancements for MSME exporters effective 01.10.2025 confirmed in Ministry of Textiles Rajya Sabha reply (20 Mar 2026)
notes: Benefit is risk cover/insurance, not a cash transfer; it lowers lenders' risk and improves credit access and pricing for MSME exporters. NIRVIK is the enhanced-cover branding. Confirm current products and premiums from ecgc.in.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Complements export finance rows (interest subvention, CGSE)

benefit_name: Additional collateral-free working capital for MSME exporters
provider_name: Ministry of Commerce and Industry (under Export Promotion Mission)
benefit_summary: Guarantee-backed additional working-capital credit for eligible exporters, particularly MSMEs, enabling collateral-free access over and above existing limits under the Export Promotion Mission.
benefit_calculation_logic: Provides additional credit support of up to 20pct of existing working-capital limits to eligible borrowers, particularly MSMEs, by enabling collateral-free access under a guarantee.
active_status_evidence: CGSE approved under the Export Promotion Mission (Ministry of Textiles Rajya Sabha reply, 20 Mar 2026); operational rollout to confirm
notes: Recently approved under the Export Promotion Mission; operational guidelines and lender onboarding should be confirmed before publication. This is the export credit-guarantee component flagged during C3.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Part of the Export Promotion Mission (Niryat Protsahan); complements the interest subvention row in C3

benefit_name: Collateral-free Enterprise Development Loan (two tranches)
benefit_summary: Collateral-free enterprise loan to registered artisans/craftspeople in 18 traditional trades, in two tranches at a concessional 5pct interest, with government interest subvention and a graded credit guarantee.
benefit_calculation_logic: Tranche 1 up to 1 lakh (18-month repayment); tranche 2 up to 2 lakh (30-month repayment) after repaying tranche 1 and completing advanced training or adopting digital transactions. Beneficiary pays 5pct; GoI provides 8pct interest subvention. CGTMSE graded portfolio guarantee.
active_status_evidence: pmvishwakarma.gov.in live; 4.7 lakh loans worth ~41,188 crore sanctioned by Aug 2025 (IBEF/PIB)
notes: Registration via Common Service Centres with Aadhaar biometrics. Basic training required before tranche 1. Not for those who took PMEGP/PM SVANidhi/MUDRA in the last 5 years. Scheme runs FY2023-24 to FY2027-28.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Also provides toolkit incentive and stipend/digital incentive (separate rows)

benefit_name: Toolkit incentive (e-voucher)
benefit_summary: One-time toolkit incentive provided as an e-RUPI voucher to registered artisans at the start of basic training, to buy modern trade-specific tools. It is a grant, not a loan.
benefit_calculation_logic: Flat 15,000 e-voucher per beneficiary, after skill verification, at the start of basic training. Usable only to purchase tools (not withdrawable as cash).
active_status_evidence: Toolkit e-voucher described in current scheme guidelines; over 6 lakh beneficiaries given tools/loans by Sep 2024 (PIB)
notes: e-RUPI voucher, tool-purchase only. Delivered after skill assessment at the start of basic training.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Component of PM Vishwakarma; see loan and stipend/digital rows

benefit_name: Training stipend and digital-transaction incentive
benefit_summary: Cash support to registered artisans: a daily stipend during skill training and a small per-transaction incentive to encourage adoption of digital payments.
benefit_calculation_logic: Training stipend of 500 per day during basic (5-7 days) and advanced (15+ days) training. Digital-transaction incentive of 1 per eligible digital transaction, capped at 100 transactions per month.
active_status_evidence: Stipend and digital-incentive components described in current scheme guidelines
notes: Combined here as two small cash incentives under one benefit. Stipend value depends on number of training days; digital incentive capped at 100/month.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Component of PM Vishwakarma; see loan and toolkit rows

benefit_name: Collateral-free invoice discounting of MSME receivables
provider_name: RBI-regulated TReDS platforms (RXIL, M1xchange, Invoicemart, KredX DTX)
benefit_summary: RBI-regulated digital platforms let MSME suppliers sell their invoices raised on large corporates, PSUs and government departments to banks/NBFCs through competitive bidding, obtaining early, collateral-free, non-recourse payment.
benefit_calculation_logic: MSME receives the discounted invoice value upfront; financiers bid competitively (reported effective rates around 5-8pct per annum). No collateral and no recourse to the MSME if the buyer defaults.
active_status_evidence: RBI-regulated; buyer onboarding mandatory for companies above 250 crore turnover and CPSEs (by 31 Mar 2025); ~1.38 lakh crore financed FY24, growing
notes: Benefit is early liquidity on receivables, not a subsidy. Requires the buyer to also be on a TReDS platform. GeM Sahay is a related invoice-based financing facility for GeM sellers (noted in methodology).
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related to invoice_discounting under SIDBI/RXIL; complements working-capital schemes

scheme_or_product_name: RBI Collateral-Free Lending Norm for Micro and Small Enterprises
benefit_name: Mandatory collateral-free credit up to 20 lakh (25 lakh discretionary)
benefit_summary: RBI regulatory mandate under which banks and NBFCs cannot demand collateral for loans to micro and small enterprises up to a specified ceiling, improving formal-credit access for asset-light small businesses.
benefit_calculation_logic: Mandatory no-collateral for MSE loans up to 20 lakh (raised from 10 lakh), effective 01 April 2026 under the Lending to MSME Sector (Amendment) Directions, 2026. Banks may extend collateral-free loans up to 25 lakh for units with a good track record and sound financials. Also applies to PMEGP-financed units. Credit guarantee (for example CGTMSE) may be used.
active_status_evidence: RBI Lending to MSME Sector (Amendment) Directions, 2026 raise the mandatory collateral-free limit to 20 lakh, effective 01 Apr 2026
notes: This is a lending norm (regulatory benefit), not a scheme with an application; it underpins collateral-free access across bank MSE lending. Voluntary pledge of gold/silver within the limit is not treated as a violation.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Interacts with CGTMSE, PMEGP, MUDRA and bank MSME products captured in other batches

benefit_name: 50pct capital subsidy for livestock entrepreneurship
provider_name: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying
benefit_summary: Credit-linked capital subsidy to entrepreneurs, FPOs, SHGs and companies for setting up livestock ventures (poultry, sheep, goat, piggery, feed and fodder units), to promote private investment and employment in animal husbandry.
benefit_calculation_logic: 50pct capital subsidy of eligible project cost, with component ceilings: up to 25 lakh (rural poultry), up to 50 lakh (sheep/goat and feed/fodder units), up to 30 lakh (piggery). Beneficiary brings a minimum share; balance via bank loan. Land, personal vehicles and working capital are excluded from subsidy.
active_status_evidence: NLM entrepreneurship component described as current (2025-26) with a live application route via udyamimitra
notes: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying scheme; relevant to agri-allied/rural MSMEs. DPR and bank sanction required. Confirm current component ceilings from DAHD guidelines.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Earlier NLM-EDEG/DEDS windows were closed; this is the current entrepreneurship component

scheme_or_product_name: NABARD Credit-Linked Capital Subsidy for Warehousing/Godowns
benefit_name: Capital subsidy for construction of agri storage/godown
benefit_summary: Credit-linked capital subsidy channelled via NABARD for construction/renovation of rural godowns and agri-storage infrastructure, benefiting agri-allied MSMEs, FPOs and rural entrepreneurs.
benefit_calculation_logic: Subsidy on eligible project cost with a permissible construction cost cap (reported around 3,000 per MT). Reported rates: 33.33pct (max about 50 lakh) for FPOs, panchayats, women, SC/ST and SHGs; 25pct (max about 37.5 lakh) for other categories.
active_status_evidence: Described as a current NABARD credit-linked capital subsidy for godown construction; figures from secondary sources
notes: Overlaps with the Agriculture Marketing Infrastructure (AMI) sub-scheme and the Agriculture Infrastructure Fund. Confirm the current operative window, rates and ceilings from NABARD.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Related to AIF and AMI (agri-storage/marketing infrastructure)

benefit_name: Financial incentives for SC/ST MSEs (reimbursements and procurement support)
provider_name: Ministry of MSME (National SC-ST Hub, implemented by NSIC)
benefit_summary: A set of financial incentives to help SC/ST-owned MSEs participate in public procurement and build capacity, including reimbursements for e-commerce membership, training and certification, and support linked to the special SC/ST capital subsidy.
benefit_calculation_logic: Includes reimbursement of e-commerce portal membership fees (for example GeM, TRIFED) at 80pct or maximum 25,000 per financial year, reimbursement of top-institute short-course fees, and other procurement-participation support. Linked to SCLCSS (25pct capital subsidy, captured in the Ministry of MSME batch).
active_status_evidence: scsthub.in describes current financial-assistance components; scheme reviewed by MSME Minister in Aug 2024
notes: Bundles several small financial incentives for SC/ST MSEs. Max shown is the e-commerce membership reimbursement cap; other components (course-fee and certification reimbursements) have separate caps to confirm. SCLCSS is the linked capital subsidy.
duplicate_or_related_scheme_notes: Linked to SCLCSS (Ministry of MSME batch) and NSIC SPRS
